title: Harnessing Nature: The Cosmeceutical Promise of Medicinal Plants
author: S. Guru Prasad*, M. Sreevani
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Recently developed speech emotion recognition (SER) methods have placed much emphasis on the derivation of features of acoustic data. In this paper, an alternative method is described, which shows a higher accuracy of the SER implementation because it does not rely on any manual steps, such as feature extraction. The suggested approach is a mixture of Conformer blocks and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) which are used to predict the emotions directly based on the audio signals. The Conformer block is used to solicit long-range dependencies and time-specific features whereas CNN layers are good at extracting localized emotional information. In this design, detailed emotional content is maintained since it is otherwise overlooked due to the conventional approaches and at the same time the hierarchical depictions of emotional content are captured. The Conformer combination of time and situation data, and the CNN layers make special attention to extracting spatial features. The model was tested on three publicly available datasets and in more than one language and significantly improved accuracy and interpretability with the use of emotional and temporal information. This method moves toward affective computing since it offers a more detailed and effective analysis of the speech data  .

In this paper, an alternative method is described, which shows a higher accuracy of the SER implementation because it does not rely on any manual steps, such as feature extraction. The suggested approach is a mixture of Conformer blocks and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) which are used to predict the emotions directly based on the audio signals. The Conformer block is used to solicit long-range dependencies and time-specific features whereas CNN layers are good at extracting localized emotional information. In this design, detailed emotional content is maintained since it is otherwise overlooked due to the conventional approaches and at the same time the hierarchical depictions of emotional content are captured. The Conformer combination of time and situation data, and the CNN layers make special attention to extracting spatial features. The model was tested on three publicly available datasets and in more than one language and significantly improved accuracy and interpretability with the use of emotional and temporal information. This method moves toward affective computing since it offers a more detailed and effective analysis of the speech data  .
keywords: Affective Computing, Speech Emotion Recognition, Deep Learning, Conformer-CNN Hybrid, Interpretability
